news.summary: Hungarian voters delivered a shock election result on Sunday, ending Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule and handing victory to pro-European challenger Péter Magyar, in a vote set to reshape Hungary’s ties with the EU and reverberate across global far-right movements.

news.headline: Pope prioritises world's fastest-growing Catholic region in major Africa tour
news.summary: Leo XIV wants the world's attention on a continent vital to the Church's future, an aide says.

news.headline: Frostbite is least of worries for Canada forces grappling with new Arctic reality
news.summary: Across 5,200 km of Canada's Arctic, some 1,300 members of Canada's military spent two winter months on a mission to meet the challenges of a changing north.

news.headline: Chinese carmakers squeeze out Asian rivals in Europe as exports top 1 million units
news.summary:
Chinese carmakers are rapidly gaining ground in Europe, squeezing out Asian rivals such as Japan and South Korea as exports to the region surge past 1 million units for the first time, according to an industry report.
Imports of Chinese-made cars into the European Union in 2025 jumped 30.7 per cent

news.headline: Trump orders naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz after failed US-Iran talks
news.summary: President Donald Trump said the US Navy will begin a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz Monday morning after US-Iran ceasefire talks in Pakistan collapsed without agreement. This announcement fuelled fears of further disruption to global energy supplies and shipping routes.

news.headline: Trump's blockade threat raises risks and leaves predicaments unchanged
news.summary: The conflict is now a test of wills - Iran’s capacity to absorb strikes versus Trump’s tolerance for the war's costs.

news.headline: Peter Magyar says his election win has ‘liberated Hungary’ from Orban
news.summary: Peter Magyar has won Hungary’s election, defeating longtime nationalist leader Viktor Orban.

news.headline: Trump announces naval blockade of Iran after Islamabad talks yield no deal
news.summary: The blockade could derail a tenuous ceasefire after just five days. Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. remains open to diplomacy if Iran takes “our final and best offer.”

news.headline: US to begin blockade of Iranian ports Monday: military
news.summary: US-Iran talks fail to find deal but Gulf truce holds for now

news.headline: Hungary Election Results: Viktor Orban Concedes Defeat and Congratulates Peter Magyar
news.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who has inspired populist movements globally, could not overcome the growing dissatisfaction of his own citizens.
